joke.raw_joke: When my wife and I divorced, the judge made us split everything evenly, including our pudding shop. — We now have shared custardy.

seed_id: seed-083

script:
  setup: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."
  punchline: "We now have shared custardy."

changes_made: Trimmed the opening 'When' to drop directly into the scenario. 'When my wife and I divorced' -> 'My wife and I divorced' saves one beat and hits faster. Punchline is untouched — 'shared custardy' is perfect construction and must not be altered. The specificity of 'pudding shop' is what sells the wordplay; keeping it.
caption_notes: Two cards. Setup: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop." Punchline: "We now have shared custardy." The em-dash in the setup can be a visual pause beat on screen.

tts_notes: 'custardy' — TTS should read this correctly as it rhymes with 'custody.' If TTS mangles it, phonetic guide: KUSS-ter-dee.

IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v1

seed_id: seed-083
concepts_count: 3
casting_note: No RILEY_CASTING_PLAN provided; Iris assigned casting inline.

Cast assignments:
1. Jerry Gold — Divorced husband / narrator (Classic Dad persona). Tells the story with mild exasperation. The divorce is an inconvenience, not a tragedy.
2. Zoe Sparks — Ex-wife (Optimistic Teacher persona). Appears in courtroom/shop scenes. Upbeat even during a divorce — that's Zoe.
3. Noah Check — Judge (Principal persona). Authority figure delivering the ruling. Noah's no-nonsense energy makes the absurd ruling feel procedural.

---

## Concept A — "The Courtroom Split"
Style: Courtroom divorce hearing, played straight, absurd ruling
Duration: 9.8s — Needs room for the setup's specificity (pudding shop) before the pun.

Master video prompt:
"Cartoon courtroom, vertical 9:16, 2.5D illustration style, clean bold lines, warm neutral tones. Noah Check (no-nonsense dad, short hair, glasses, dark suit) at judge's bench with gavel. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) and Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, teal jacket) seated at separate tables on either side of the aisle. Between them on the courtroom floor: a large cartoon pudding cup or custard bowl as an exhibit. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.0s): HOOK — The Courtroom
A cartoon courtroom interior with wooden paneling and overhead fluorescent lights. At the elevated judge's bench sits Noah Check — a sturdy, no-nonsense man with short cropped hair, rectangular glasses, wearing a dark suit under judicial robes. He holds a gavel and looks down at the proceedings with absolute seriousness. Below him, seated at separate wooden tables on either side of an aisle, are Jerry Gold (left table — brown hair, handlebar mustache, teal jacket, looking mildly inconvenienced) and Zoe Sparks (right table — orange-magenta two-tone hair, teal glasses, teal jacket with star pin, sitting upright with optimistic composure). On the floor between their tables sits a comically large ceramic bowl of yellow custard pudding with a spoon sticking out of it — a court exhibit. The absurdity of the exhibit is played completely straight.

Scene 2 (2.0–5.8s): SETUP — The Ruling
Same courtroom. Noah Check at the judge's bench (glasses, dark suit, robes) reads from a document with bureaucratic authority, one finger tracing the text. Jerry Gold at his table (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) has slumped slightly, elbow on the table, chin resting on his hand — the posture of a man enduring a process. Zoe Sparks at her table (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses) sits perfectly upright, hands folded, nodding along as if the divorce proceedings are going quite well actually. The large custard bowl exhibit is still on the floor between them. Courtroom lighting, neutral tones.
Caption: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."

Scene 3 (5.8–6.3s): THE PAUSE
Same courtroom, framing focused on Jerry Gold at his table. He looks directly at the viewer. His expression is flat resignation — the look of a man who has accepted his new reality. The custard bowl is visible in the lower portion of the frame. Noah Check's gavel is visible at the top. One beat of silence. He's about to explain the arrangement.

Scene 4 (6.3–9.8s): PUNCHLINE — The Arrangement
Same courtroom. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) at his table, looking at viewer with deadpan acceptance. Behind him, the courtroom scene continues — Noah Check at the bench has set down his gavel, ruling delivered. Zoe Sparks at her table has both hands clasped together near her face in delight, beaming, as if "shared custardy" is actually a wonderful arrangement. The large custard bowl between the tables has been visually split down the middle — a literal line drawn through it, one half tilting toward Jerry's side, the other toward Zoe's. The absurdity is total but everyone in the room treats it as procedurally normal.
Caption: "We now have shared custardy."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.0s no text | 2.0–5.8s setup | 6.3–9.8s punchline
Voice: Jerry mild exasperation for setup. 0.5s pause. Punchline flat and resigned — he's accepted this reality. Emphasize nothing; the pun does the work. Noah's ruling voice is only implied, not heard.
Risk: LOW. "Custardy" might confuse TTS — phonetic guide: KUSS-ter-dee. Fallback: remove courtroom, just Jerry at a kitchen table with pudding cups explaining his divorce settlement.
Jerry hook: "Three cast members, courtroom drama, a giant pudding exhibit, and a pun that gets better the more you think about it. Noah as judge is casting gold."
Vince: 9:16, 9.8s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+split custard+Zoe delight, Scene 1 courtroom establish with pudding exhibit], trims=[Scene 3 can shorten to 0.3s]

---

## Concept B — "The Pudding Shop"
Style: Two ex-spouses operating a divided pudding shop, sitcom energy
Duration: 9.2s — Punchy domestic scene.

Master video prompt:
"Colorful pudding shop interior, cartoon 2.5D illustration, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. The shop is literally divided down the middle by a thick line of tape on the floor. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket, apron) stands on the left side. Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, teal jacket, apron) stands on the right side. Each has their own cash register, their own pudding display case. A sign reads 'PUDDING PALACE' but the middle letters have been split. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.2s): HOOK — The Divided Shop
A bright, cheerful pudding shop interior with pastel-colored walls, display cases full of colorful custards and puddings, and warm lighting. Down the exact center of the floor runs a thick strip of blue painter's tape, dividing the shop perfectly in half. On the left side stands Jerry Gold — a middle-aged man with brown hair, handlebar mustache, teal jacket under a white apron — leaning against his half of the counter with his arms crossed. On the right side stands Zoe Sparks — a young woman with orange-magenta two-tone hair, teal glasses, teal jacket with star pin under her own white apron — cheerfully arranging pudding cups in her display case. Above them, a shop sign reads "PUDDING PALACE" but the sign itself has been sawed in half down the middle, each half hanging at a slightly different angle. The tape line runs through everything — counter, floor, ceiling.

Scene 2 (2.2–5.6s): SETUP — The Explanation
Same divided pudding shop. Jerry Gold on his side (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket, white apron) faces the viewer, gesturing behind him at the divided shop with one hand while the other hand holds a pudding cup. His expression is weary exasperation — this is his life now. Behind him on his side of the shop, his display case has puddings arranged somewhat haphazardly. On the other side of the tape line, Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, apron) serves a blurred customer with a big smile, her side noticeably more organized and decorated. The blue tape line is prominent between them. Warm shop lighting.
Caption: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."

Scene 3 (5.6–6.0s): THE BEAT
Same shop, closer framing on Jerry Gold at his counter (brown hair, mustache, apron). He sighs. The pudding cup is still in his hand. The blue tape line is visible at the edge of frame. He's about to name the arrangement.

Scene 4 (6.0–9.2s): PUNCHLINE — The Name
Same divided pudding shop. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, apron) looks at the viewer deadpan, pudding cup raised slightly as if toasting his new reality. On the other side of the tape, Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, apron) has turned to look at him through the divide — she's grinning, both hands clasped near her chin. She clearly finds the arrangement delightful. The sign above them — "PUDDING PALACE" split in half — is visible. Jerry's side of the shop is titled in slightly dimmer lighting. Zoe's side is brighter. The visual metaphor is obvious and deliberate.
Caption: "We now have shared custardy."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.2s no text | 2.2–5.6s setup | 6.0–9.2s punchline
Voice: Jerry weary, resigned. 0.5s pause. Punchline delivered as stating a fact of his new life. No emphasis.
Risk: LOW. The divided shop is visually complex but all in one location — AI gen should handle it. Fallback: simplify to just Jerry and Zoe standing on opposite sides of a table with pudding between them, tape line visible.
Jerry hook: "The divided shop is instantly funny before anyone speaks. The visual gag and the verbal pun work independently AND together. Double payload."
Vince: 9:16, 9.2s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+Zoe grin, Scene 1 divided shop reveal], trims=[Scene 3 cut to 0.2s]

## Concept C [SURREAL] — "The Custard Gavel"
Style: Courtroom where everything is made of pudding — surreal escalation
Duration: 10.0s — Extra time for the transformation beat.

surreal_element: The courtroom itself transforms into pudding. The gavel is a spoon. The bench is a giant custard bowl. The ruling literally creates shared custody by splitting a building-sized pudding in half, which floods the courtroom.
Weirdness budget: 7/10 — Controlled absurdity. The characters react normally to an impossible situation.

Master video prompt:
"A courtroom that is gradually transforming into pudding, cartoon 2.5D illustration, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. The wooden judge's bench has become a giant ceramic custard bowl. Noah Check (short hair, glasses, dark suit, judge robes) stands inside the bowl-bench wielding a giant spoon as a gavel. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) and Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses) stand knee-deep in flowing yellow custard that has flooded the courtroom floor. Everyone treats this as normal procedure."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.5s): HOOK — Normal Courtroom (Grounded)
A normal cartoon courtroom — wooden paneling, fluorescent lighting, American flag in the corner. Noah Check sits at the judge's bench — a sturdy man with short hair, rectangular glasses, wearing a dark suit under black judicial robes. He holds a standard wooden gavel. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) and Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, teal jacket) stand at separate podiums. Everything looks completely ordinary. Professional. Procedural. But on the judge's bench, next to Noah's papers, sits a single pudding cup. Nobody has acknowledged it.
Caption: None.

Scene 2 (2.5–5.5s): SETUP — The Ruling
Same courtroom, but something has shifted. The wooden judge's bench has begun to warp, its edges rounding and glazing over — it is becoming a giant ceramic custard bowl. Noah Check (glasses, dark suit, robes) still sits in it, unbothered, reading his ruling from a document that is now slightly sticky. His wooden gavel has become a large serving spoon, which he holds naturally. Jerry Gold at his podium (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) looks mildly concerned — not about the pudding transformation, but about the divorce ruling. Zoe Sparks at her podium (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses) hasn't noticed anything unusual. The floor near the bench has a thin layer of yellow custard seeping outward. The American flag in the corner is starting to droop, as if made of caramel.
Caption: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."

Scene 3 (5.5–6.5s): TRANSFORMATION BEAT — The Split
The courtroom has fully transformed. Noah Check stands inside what is now a building-sized ceramic custard bowl that used to be the judge's bench, waist-deep in yellow pudding, holding his spoon-gavel high above his head. He brings it down in a dramatic strike — the giant custard bowl CRACKS down the middle, a clean line splitting it in two. Yellow custard floods outward in a wave across the courtroom floor. Jerry Gold and Zoe Sparks stand at their podiums, now knee-deep in flowing custard. Jerry looks down at the custard with tired acceptance. Zoe looks delighted, arms spread wide as if welcoming the flood. The courtroom walls are dripping with yellow pudding. Noah's expression has not changed. This is standard procedure.

Scene 4 (6.5–10.0s): PUNCHLINE — Shared Custardy
The courtroom is now fully flooded with thick yellow custard up to everyone's waists. The cracked custard-bowl bench has split into two perfect halves — one tilting toward Jerry's side, one toward Zoe's side. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket, standing in waist-deep custard) looks at the viewer. His expression is complete deadpan resignation. A pudding cup floats past him in the custard. On the other side, Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, standing in custard) is scooping pudding with her hands and tasting it, genuinely happy. Noah Check in the background, standing between the two halves of the cracked bowl, holds up a document — the custody agreement — now dripping with custard. Yellow pudding covers every surface. Everyone is calm.
Caption: "We now have shared custardy."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.5s no text | 2.5–5.5s setup | 6.5–10.0s punchline
Transformation beat: 5.5–6.5s — Noah's spoon-gavel cracks the bowl-bench, flooding the courtroom with custard. Reality fully breaks.
Voice: Jerry weary. Noah authoritative, procedural. 0.5s pause. Punchline flat resignation. The chaos around him is not acknowledged in his delivery.
Risk: MEDIUM. Complex surreal scene may render inconsistently. Anthropomorphic furniture + fluid dynamics = AI gen challenge. Fallback: hard cut from normal courtroom (Scenes 1-2) to fully-flooded courtroom (Scene 4) — skip the transformation, let the contrast carry it.
Jerry hook: "This one's weird on purpose. The AI will make it weirder. That's the bit. But the pun still works even if you strip all the pudding — that's how you know it's a good joke."
Vince: 9:16, 10.0s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 flooded courtroom punchline, Scene 3 transformation, Scene 1 normal courtroom for contrast], trims=[Scene 1 can shorten to 1.5s]
